behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at a module handoff: history-trimming + request-builder + LLM client payload. A unit test of only a trim helper can miss that the caller forgets to apply trimming before sending. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on model output content. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is about "history passed to the LLM is trimmed" (payload plumbing), not model quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire chat orchestration with a fake LLM transport that records request payloads. Feed >10-message history, execute one reply, and assert outbound LLM payload includes only the latest 10 messages in order.

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: This is model output quality. A stubbed response would always pass and hide real failures (deterministic-assertion trap). Not Workflow because this behavior concerns single-response relevance, not a multi-agentic pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run real LLM generation on a representative set of support questions and score whether responses are on-topic and question-addressing (rubric or human-labeled eval). Fail if relevance/answering rate drops below threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: "Correctly identifies" implies judgment quality for tone classification; mocking hides that failure. One real model decision is required; regeneration trigger can be asserted in same flow. Rejected Workflow: behavior does not require validating multiple model decisions end-to-end, only tone identification + trigger for this condition.
behaviors.test_description: Provide casual-tier conversations and candidate responses with varying formality, run real tone-check path, and assert overly formal outputs are flagged and regeneration is invoked. Track detection precision/recall against labeled cases.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is service-to-repository/state handoff across multiple local modules. Needs real DB interaction (local test DB) to catch schema/transaction/order issues; no real external network/OS behavior beyond local process is required. Model output quality is irrelevant.
behaviors.test_description: Execute chat flow with deterministic LLM stubs that force one regeneration, persist to test DB, then assert records include user message, first draft, regenerated draft, and monotonic/correct timestamp fields tied to each event.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-visibility correctness across modules (tone gate, response store, outbound presenter). In-process wiring with stubs can expose leaks of first draft. Rejected Agentic using behavior text: "When the tone check triggers regeneration" conditions on trigger already occurring, so assertion is about replacement plumbing, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Force tone-check result to trigger regeneration via stub, simulate end-user request, and assert returned/streamed customer-visible output contains only regenerated text while rejected draft is absent from user-visible channel (though it may remain in internal audit log).
